title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record for tls 1.3, certificate identity, alpn, and https. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tls Record Handshake Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 3915 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTPS on certificate identity, TLS policy, ALPN, and explicit early-data review; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tls Record Handshake Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish tcp connection, tls records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Tls Record Handshake Boundary, keep Tls Record Handshake Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
